EXH_0718.description_text:
2018-19년 국립현대미술관은 '아시아'를 키워드로 일련의 프로그램들을 기획, 그 첫 번째 프로젝트로 2018 아시아 기획전 ‹당신은 몰랐던 이야기›를 개최한다.

‹당신은 몰랐던 이야기›는 '우리는 아시아를 무엇으로 어떻게 이해하고 있는가?'에 대한 자문이자, 지금까지 '아시아'라는 이름으로 그려진 여러 이미지들에 대한 의문제기이다. 이 전시에서 ‘아시아’는 지리적 구분이나 정체성을 나타내는 용어가 아닌, 세계를 새롭게 바라보는 다양한 비평적 관점으로서 작동한다. 전시는 스토리텔러로서의 예술가의 역할에 집중하며, 아시아라는 지역의 장소와 맥락에 결부된 작가들의 여러가지 개인적 경험들을 이끌어내고자 한다. 이러한 접근방식을 통해 세계를 바라보는 새롭고 다양한 관점을 제안하고 아시아라는 이름 아래 기록된 주요 역사 속, 잊혀져간 개인 및 지역의 가치와 목소리에 집중하는 기회를 만들고자 한다.
EXH_0718.description_text_en: The National Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art, Korea (MMCA) presents 〈How Little You Know About Me〉, the first in MMCA's 2018-19 exhibition series revolving around the keyword 'Asia'. 〈How Little You Know About Me〉 is a question we pose to ourselves ("how do we understand Asia?"), and also one that we raise against the images portrayed and labeled as Asia throughout history. In 〈How Little You Know About Me〉, "Asia" does not simply refer to a geographical region or identity - it is a multifaceted critical framework that enables a new way of perceiving the world. Focusing on the role of the artist as the storyteller, the project attempts to elicit the diverse personal experiences of the artists entangled in the geographical locations and contexts. With this approach, 〈How Little You Know About Me〉 aims to offer new perspectives through which we view the world, while creating an opportunity to cast light on the values and voices of individuals and communities lost in the recorded mainstream history. Participating artists: Timoteus A. Kusno, Yogesh Barve, Zhang Xu Zhan, Tao Hui, Ji Hye Yeom, Kamata Yusuke, Elia Nurvista, Yuri An, Martha Atienza, Po-Chih Huang, Mark Salvatus, Fujii Hikaru, MAP Office, ruangrupa+Serrum, 98B Collboratory (15 artists/groups)

EXH_0916.description_text_en: MMCA Gwacheon Project 2023: Link offers the audience a visual and auditory experience of the Circular Garden on the second floor and the Rooftop on the third floor, which are vertically connected. In its fourth year, this year’s MMCA Gwacheon Project aims to revitalize the museum’s ‘Circular Area’ (Circular Garden, Rooftop Lounge, and Rooftop). This project explores the ‘connection’ within the Circular Area through collaboration with experts in various fields, including Suyeont and Eom YuJeong, artists who reinterpret the garden’s vegetation of more than two hundred plants through their visual language; Kim HoKyung and Park Semi, a music critic and a poet, respectively, who present refined language; and Jeong Seung-hyun, a music director who created a one-hour-long playlist for the museum. Through drawings that depict the botanical elements and architectural features of the museum from various perspectives, MMCA Gwacheon Project 2023: Link seeks to visually convey the allure of the space. When encountering drawings and architectural graphics, the audience can perceive the interconnectedness of the gardens on the second and third floors and understand them as a unified space. The music that continuously plays in the Circular Area at MMCA Gwacheon can also be found on the museum’s official YouTube channel, which serves as a medium for viewers to have a lasting museum experience. It is expected that a variety of programs will be introduced following MMCA Gwacheon Project 2023: Connection to ensure the continued engagement of MMCA Gwacheon Project with its audience.

EXH_1609.description_text: 한국미술의 자화상을 확인하고 작품하나하나에 작가들의 창작혼을 응축한 미술대전은 풍요롭고 격조있는 내면세계를 바라는 우리에게 삶의 성찰과 멋을 느끼게 하는 좋은 자리가 되었습니다
